demographics.living_situation: at home in own residence; was alone at the time of the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is tight, my heart will not stop pounding, and I read my portal results saying I have a blood protein linked to bone cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about two hours ago right after I opened my blood test results on the portal — I got a message flagging an abnormal protein and I panicked and now my chest is tight and my heart is racing.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, right after I read my blood test results on the patient portal
symptom_narrative.character: tight feeling in my chest, heart pounding and racing, breathing fast
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels really bad and I'm frightened, though the tightness eases off when I slow my breathing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: in the middle of my chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on suddenly and has been there since, but it settles a bit when I calm down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: slowing my breathing down helps, and it eased when I stopped looking at the results
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the cancer protein result and reading more about it online
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel like I can't catch my breath and a bit lightheaded from breathing so fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't settle, I've been pacing around the house

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an abnormal blood protein picked up on a routine blood test
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: found on a routine screening blood test; I only saw it on my portal today
past_medical_history.how_managed: nothing yet — I haven't spoken to my doctor about it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: got very anxious after reading the portal result and started searching about the protein online

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants firm reassurance that this does not mean they have bone cancer

communication_style.typical_message_style: worried, keeps returning to the cancer result, asks repeated reassurance questions

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's pounding and racing really fast
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: the patient's subjective sense of a fast heartbeat, not a counted or device-measured rate

objective_data_offered.value: I'm breathing really fast and feel like I can't catch my breath
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported hyperventilation during acute distress; not a measured respiratory rate or oxygen level
